description: Fix infinite carousel loop + card snap-to-center

userStories.title: Fix: infinite carousel loop stops at the end instead of looping
userStories.description: As a visitor, the carousel should loop forever without stopping.
acceptanceCriteria: In the carousel script block (second <script> tag), replace `var halfWidth = carousel.scrollWidth / 2;` with: `var origCards2 = carousel.querySelectorAll('.card'); var loopWidth = origCards2[origCards2.length / 2].offsetLeft;` — this measures the offsetLeft of the FIRST CLONE card (index origCount), which is the exact pixel width of one full set including gaps and leading padding
Replace the reset check `if (carousel.scrollLeft >= halfWidth) { carousel.scrollLeft -= halfWidth; }` with `if (carousel.scrollLeft >= loopWidth) { carousel.scrollLeft -= loopWidth; }`
userStories.notes: Root cause: scrollWidth / 2 includes padding-right once and padding-left once, so halfWidth != one set width. The first clone's offsetLeft is the exact point where the second set starts, making it the correct reset threshold.

userStories.title: Fix: clicking a card should snap it to center of the carousel viewport
userStories.description: As a visitor, when I click a card image or 'Read more', the carousel should smooth-scroll so that card is horizontally centered in view.
acceptanceCriteria: In the card click handler (first <script> tag), replace the setTimeout with scrollIntoView with: `setTimeout(function () { var target = card.offsetLeft - carousel.offsetLeft - (carousel.clientWidth / 2 - card.offsetWidth / 2); carousel.scrollTo({ left: target, behavior: 'smooth' }); }, 40);`
The scrollTo must operate on the `workCarousel` element (the overflow container), NOT call scrollIntoView on the card element — scrollIntoView scrolls the page, not the carousel
The variable `carousel` is not in scope in the first script block — use `workCarousel` which IS in scope there
userStories.notes: Root cause: scrollIntoView() acts on the nearest scrollable ancestor UP the DOM, which on mobile/desktop can be the page itself rather than the carousel. Manual carousel.scrollTo() with computed offsetLeft is reliable.
